Such rooms are forced-node \\\"gateways\\\"\\n// regardless of their exit count so the routing graph always has a node on\\n// each side of the unique inter-quadrant passage.\\nfunction isLabRoutingGatewayRoom(state, room, context) {\\n  if (!room?.exits || !context) return false\\n  for (const [dir, delta] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    if (room.exits[dir] !== true) continue\\n    if (getLabBoundaryCrossingSegmentKey(room.x, room.y, delta.dx, delta.dy, context)) return true\\n  }\\n  return false\\n}\\n\\n// True when `room` has at least one open exit pointing to a neighbour that\\n// is either unrecorded or recorded but not yet explored.  Such rooms are\\n// \\\"frontier parents\\\" — they sit at the boundary between explored corridor\\n// and unexplored maze.  We force them to be routing nodes so that every\\n// frontier room has a real graph node attached to it via a single-edge\\n// stub, which means Dijkstra over the routing graph can always answer the\\n// \\\"nearest frontier from here\\\" question without falling back to BFS.\\nfunction hasLabUnexploredNeighbor(state, room) {\\n  if (!room?.exits) return false\\n  for (const [dir, delta] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    if (room.exits[dir] !== true) continue\\n    const nb = state.rooms?.[getLabKey(room.x + delta.dx, room.y + delta.dy)]\\n    if (!nb || !nb.explored) return true\\n  }\\n  return false\\n}\\n\\n// Decide whether `room` is a routing node.  Junction (>=3 exits), dead-end\\n// (<=1 exits), any gateway, AND any frontier-parent (has unexplored\\n// neighbour) all count.  Only plain 2-exit corridor cells whose both\\n// neighbours are explored remain corridors.\\nfunction isLabRoutingNodeRoom(state, room, context = getLabInfluenceContext(state)) {\\n  const n = countLabRoomExits(room)\\n  if (n < 0) return false\\n  if (n !== 2) return true\\n  if (isLabRoutingGatewayRoom(state, room, context)) return true\\n  if (hasLabUnexploredNeighbor(state, room)) return true\\n  return false\\n}\\n\\n// Subnet identifier for a coord — one of the four quadrant keys.  \\\"\\\" when\\n// outside any quadrant (shouldn't happen post-fix).\\nfunction getLabRoutingSubnetForCoords(x, y, context) {\\n  if (!context) return \\\"\\\"\\n  const bounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(x, y, context)\\n  return bounds?.key ?? \\\"\\\"\\n}\\n\\n// Canonical key for an edge identified by its two endpoint room keys.\\n// Sorted so the same pair always hashes to the same key regardless of which\\n// end called.\\nfunction labRoutingEdgePairKey(aKey, bKey) {\\n  return aKey < bKey ? `${aKey}|${bKey}` : `${bKey}|${aKey}`\\n}\\n\\nfunction addLabRoutingNodeIndex(routing, nodeKey) {\\n  if (!routing.nodeEdges.has(nodeKey)) routing.nodeEdges.set(nodeKey, new Set())\\n}\\n\\n// Add a node to the routing graph (or refresh its metadata).  Idempotent —\\n// calling for a room that's already a node just refreshes the metadata.\\nfunction addLabRoutingNode(state, room, context) {\\n  const routing = ensureLabRouting(state)\\n  const key = getLabKey(room.x, room.y)\\n  routing.nodes.set(key, {\\n    x: room.x,\\n    y: room.y,\\n    exitCount: countLabRoomExits(room),\\n    subnet: getLabRoutingSubnetForCoords(room.x, room.y, context),\\n    isGateway: isLabRoutingGatewayRoom(state, room, context)\\n  })\\n  addLabRoutingNodeIndex(routing, key)\\n  // If the room had previously been classified as a corridor cell of some\\n  // edge, that edge needs to be SPLIT at this room.  Caller (the corridor\\n  // sweep) handles that.\\n}\\n\\nfunction removeLabRoutingEdge(state, edgeId) {\\n  const routing = state?.routing\\n  if (!routing) return\\n  const edge = routing.edges.get(edgeId)\\n  if (!edge) return\\n  routing.edges.delete(edgeId)\\n  routing.edgeBetween.delete(labRoutingEdgePairKey(edge.aKey, edge.bKey))\\n  routing.nodeEdges.get(edge.aKey)?.delete(edgeId)\\n  routing.nodeEdges.get(edge.bKey)?.delete(edgeId)\\n  for (const r of edge.rooms) {\\n    if (routing.roomToEdge.get(r) === edgeId) routing.roomToEdge.delete(r)\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// Add (or replace) an edge between two nodes.  Replaces any existing edge\\n// connecting the same pair, so this is also the entry point for \\\"the\\n// shortest known corridor between A and B is now this one.\\\"  An edge\\n// crossing two different subnets is automatically marked as a gateway.\\nfunction addLabRoutingEdge(state, aKey, bKey, dirsFromA, rooms, context) {\\n  if (aKey === bKey) return null\\n  const routing = ensureLabRouting(state)\\n  const pairKey = labRoutingEdgePairKey(aKey, bKey)\\n  const existingId = routing.edgeBetween.get(pairKey)\\n  if (existingId !== undefined) removeLabRoutingEdge(state, existingId)\\n  const edgeId = routing.nextEdgeId++\\n  const aSubnet = routing.nodes.get(aKey)?.subnet ?? \\\"\\\"\\n  const bSubnet = routing.nodes.get(bKey)?.subnet ?? \\\"\\\"\\n  const isGateway = aSubnet !== \\\"\\\" && bSubnet !== \\\"\\\" && aSubnet !== bSubnet\\n  const dirs = dirsFromA.slice()\\n  // Canonicalise so the stored edge is always from \\\"lower key\\\" to \\\"higher\\n  // key\\\" — easier to reason about during traversal.\\n  let storedAKey = aKey, storedBKey = bKey, storedDirs = dirs, storedRooms = rooms.slice()\\n  if (aKey > bKey) {\\n    storedAKey = bKey\\n    storedBKey = aKey\\n    storedDirs = dirs.map((d) => labDirections[d]?.back ?? d).reverse()\\n    storedRooms = rooms.slice().reverse()\\n  }\\n  routing.edges.set(edgeId, {\\n    aKey: storedAKey,\\n    bKey: storedBKey,\\n    dirsFromA: storedDirs,\\n    rooms: storedRooms,\\n    cost: storedDirs.length,\\n    isGateway\\n  })\\n  routing.edgeBetween.set(pairKey, edgeId)\\n  addLabRoutingNodeIndex(routing, storedAKey)\\n  addLabRoutingNodeIndex(routing, storedBKey)\\n  routing.nodeEdges.get(storedAKey).add(edgeId)\\n  routing.nodeEdges.get(storedBKey).add(edgeId)\\n  for (const r of storedRooms) routing.roomToEdge.set(r, edgeId)\\n  return edgeId\\n}\\n\\n// Walk a corridor starting from `fromKey` in direction `dir`.  Stops when\\n// reaching a routing node, an unexplored cell, a dead-end, or after looping\\n// back on itself.  Returns:\\n//   { result: \\\"node\\\",       endKey, dirs:[...], rooms:[...] }\\n//   { result: \\\"unexplored\\\", lastKey, dirs:[...], rooms:[...] }\\n//   { result: \\\"loop\\\"|\\\"none\\\" }\\n// `dirs` is the dir sequence used (excluding the initial step from fromKey?\\n// no — including it, so dirs[0] === dir).  `rooms` is the corridor cells\\n// passed through, excluding both endpoints.\\nfunction walkLabRoutingCorridor(state, fromKey, dir, context = getLabInfluenceContext(state)) {\\n  const fromRoom = state.rooms?.[fromKey]\\n  if (!fromRoom?.explored || fromRoom.exits?.[dir] !== true) return { result: \\\"none\\\" }\\n  const dirs = []\\n  const rooms = []\\n  let prevX = fromRoom.x, prevY = fromRoom.y\\n  let curDir = dir\\n  const visited = new Set([fromKey])\\n  while (true) {\\n    const delta = labDirections[curDir]\\n    if (!delta) return { result: \\\"none\\\" }\\n    const nextX = prevX + delta.dx\\n    const nextY = prevY + delta.dy\\n    const nextKey = getLabKey(nextX, nextY)\\n    dirs.push(curDir)\\n    if (visited.has(nextKey)) return { result: \\\"loop\\\" }\\n    visited.add(nextKey)\\n    const nextRoom = state.rooms?.[nextKey]\\n    if (!nextRoom?.explored) {\\n      // Unexplored — corridor stops here.  Drop the (still-unknown) endpoint\\n      // from the return so it isn't included in `rooms`.\\n      return { result: \\\"unexplored\\\", lastKey: nextKey, dirs, rooms: rooms.slice() }\\n    }\\n    if (isLabRoutingNodeRoom(state, nextRoom, context)) {\\n      return { result: \\\"node\\\", endKey: nextKey, dirs, rooms: rooms.slice() }\\n    }\\n    // Plain corridor cell with exactly two open exits — follow the OTHER one\\n    // (the one that isn't where we came from).\\n    rooms.push(nextKey)\\n    const backDir = labDirections[curDir].back\\n    let nextDir = \\\"\\\"\\n    for (const [d2] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n      if (d2 === backDir) continue\\n      if (nextRoom.exits[d2] !== true) continue\\n      nextDir = d2\\n      break\\n    }\\n    if (!nextDir) return { result: \\\"none\\\" }\\n    prevX = nextX\\n    prevY = nextY\\n    curDir = nextDir\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// Re-stitch the corridor passing through `room` in directions ±dir, then\\n// install (or replace) the resulting edge in the routing graph.\\nfunction rebuildLabRoutingEdgesAround(state, room, context) {\\n  const roomKey = getLabKey(room.x, room.y)\\n  const routing = ensureLabRouting(state)\\n  const isNode = isLabRoutingNodeRoom(state, room, context)\\n  // For every open exit, walk to find the node at that end of the corridor.\\n  // If the room itself is a node, the \\\"near end\\\" is the room.  If the room\\n  // is a corridor cell, the near end is whatever node lives at the other\\n  // direction.\\n  for (const [dir] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    if (room.exits?.[dir] !== true) continue\\n    if (isNode) {\\n      // Walk from this node in dir to find the far node.\\n      const walk = walkLabRoutingCorridor(state, roomKey, dir, context)\\n      if (walk.result === \\\"node\\\") {\\n        addLabRoutingEdge(state, roomKey, walk.endKey, [dir, ...walk.dirs.slice(1) ? walk.dirs.slice(1) : []].length === walk.dirs.length ? walk.dirs : [dir, ...walk.dirs.slice(1)], walk.rooms, context)\\n      }\\n      // unexplored / loop / none → no edge to install yet; will resolve when\\n      // the unexplored end is recorded.\\n    } else {\\n      // Corridor cell.  Walk in dir to find one node, walk in -dir to find\\n      // the other.  When both ends are nodes, install the merged edge.\\n      const back = labDirections[dir].back\\n      // We need to find the corridor endpoints by walking from the corridor\\n      // cell.  Since walk starts at the cell and steps in dir, we get one\\n      // end via dir and the other via back.\\n      const forward = walkLabRoutingCorridor(state, roomKey, dir, context)\\n      const backward = walkLabRoutingCorridor(state, roomKey, back, context)\\n      if (forward.result === \\\"node\\\" && backward.result === \\\"node\\\") {\\n        // Reconstruct dir sequence from backward.endKey to forward.endKey\\n        // through `room`.  Walk = reverse(backward.dirs) (each flipped)\\n        // then forward.dirs.\\n        const reverseBack = backward.dirs.map((d) => labDirections[d]?.back ?? d).reverse()\\n        // The corridor cells along the way: reverse(backward.rooms) + [roomKey]\\n        // + forward.rooms\\n        const dirs = reverseBack.concat(forward.dirs)\\n        const rooms = backward.rooms.slice().reverse().concat([roomKey], forward.rooms)\\n        addLabRoutingEdge(state, backward.endKey, forward.endKey, dirs, rooms, context)\\n      }\\n      // Only one direction touches a node yet — defer.\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// Refresh the frontier-stub index for a single explored room.  For every\\n// open exit of `room` whose neighbour is UNEXPLORED, walk back from `room`\\n// to the nearest routing node and record the full attach path on\\n// routing.frontierStubs[neighborKey].  Re-running this for the same room\\n// safely overwrites any prior stub (we always pick the cheapest).\\nfunction refreshLabRoutingFrontierStubsAround(state, room, context) {\\n  const routing = ensureLabRouting(state)\\n  if (!routing) return\\n  const roomKey = getLabKey(room.x, room.y)\\n  // Determine where (room.x, room.y) attaches in the routing graph — this\\n  // is what every frontier neighbour will share as its \\\"approach path\\\".\\n  const attach = nearestLabRoutingNodeFromRoom(state, room.x, room.y)\\n  if (!attach) return\\n  const layout = state.layout\\n  const wallCache = getLabWallCache(state, context)\\n  for (const [dir, delta] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    if (room.exits?.[dir] !== true) continue\\n    const nextX = room.x + delta.dx\\n    const nextY = room.y + delta.dy\\n    if (layout && (nextX < layout.minX || nextX > layout.maxX\\n      || nextY < layout.minY || nextY > layout.maxY)) continue\\n    const nextKey = getLabKey(nextX, nextY)\\n    const nextRoom = state.rooms?.[nextKey]\\n    if (!nextRoom || nextRoom.explored) {\\n      // Either explored (covered by the edge graph) or absent (no frontier).\\n      // If absent, also drop any stale stub that may linger.\\n      if (!nextRoom) routing.frontierStubs.delete(nextKey)\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    // Skip stubs that would step into a branch the wall analysis has\\n    // already proven to be a sealed pocket.  ONLY apply this when the\\n    // step stays inside the source quadrant — a cross-quadrant exit is\\n    // by definition the unique passage into another subnet (possibly the\\n    // only known way into the goal quadrant), never an enclosed pocket.\\n    // Evicting those breaks pathfinding into the exit subquadrant.\\n    let isDead = false\\n    if (!labDisableDeadRoomDetection) {\\n      const positiveExitBranch = isLabPositiveExitGoalSubquadrantBranch(state, room.x, room.y, dir, context)\\n      const sourceBounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(room.x, room.y, context)\\n      const stepInsideQuadrant = sourceBounds && isLabCoordsInsideBounds(nextX, nextY, sourceBounds)\\n    // HARD GUARD: never evict a frontier that lies inside the exit\\n    // subquadrant.  The exit subquadrant rooms can look \\\"enclosed\\\" to the\\n    // wall flood-fill (it's a small region near the corner with three\\n    // walls forming an L, and the only entrance can come from any side).\\n    // Marking that entrance dead strands the worker on the wrong side of\\n    // the maze with no way to reach the exit.\\n      const targetInExitSubquadrant = isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(nextX, nextY, context)\\n    // EXTRA HARD GUARD: never evict the exit cell itself, regardless of\\n    // anything else.  The exit cell is by construction reachable; if the\\n    // wall analysis says otherwise, the wall analysis is wrong.\\n      const isExitCell = layout && Number.isInteger(layout.finishX) && Number.isInteger(layout.finishY)\\n        && nextX === layout.finishX && nextY === layout.finishY\\n    // modeMapOnly: never evict a \\\"blocked\\\" frontier — mapping mode\\n    // explicitly wants those pockets visited.\\n      isDead = !isExitCell\\n        && stepInsideQuadrant\\n        && !positiveExitBranch\\n        && !targetInExitSubquadrant\\n        && isLabBranchDeadByWallState(state, room.x, room.y, dir, context, wallCache, { bounds: sourceBounds, targetX: nextX, targetY: nextY })\\n    }\\n    // (room → nextKey) is the final step into the frontier.  Path from the\\n    // attach node to `room` is attach.dirs reversed-and-flipped (because\\n    // attach.dirs walks ROOM → NODE; we want NODE → ROOM).\\n    const dirsFromNode = attach.dirs.slice().reverse().map((d) => labDirections[d]?.back ?? d)\\n    const cost = dirsFromNode.length + 1\\n    // Mark the stub as a dead-branch entrance rather than deleting it.\\n    // The picker keeps it as a low-priority option for stuck workers; it\\n    // ranks below every \\\"live\\\" frontier but is still claimable when no\\n    // better option exists.\\n    const stub = {\\n      parentNodeKey: attach.nodeKey,\\n      parentRoomKey: roomKey,\\n      dirsFromNode,\\n      finalDir: dir,\\n      cost,\\n      subnet: getLabRoutingSubnetForCoords(nextX, nextY, context),\\n      deadBranch: isDead\\n    }\\n    const existing = routing.frontierStubs.get(nextKey)\\n    // Prefer a stub with deadBranch=false over one with true even if the\\n    // false-stub costs more — a \\\"live\\\" approach to a frontier should\\n    // never be downgraded by a \\\"dead\\\" approach.  Otherwise pick by cost.\\n    if (!existing\\n      || (existing.deadBranch && !stub.deadBranch)\\n      || (existing.deadBranch === stub.deadBranch && stub.cost < existing.cost)) {\\n      routing.frontierStubs.set(nextKey, stub)\\n      setLabFrontierDeadBranchStatus(state, nextKey, stub.deadBranch)\\n    } else {\\n      setLabFrontierDeadBranchStatus(state, nextKey, existing.deadBranch)\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction refreshLabDeadBranchStatusesForFrontierStubs(state, context = getLabInfluenceContext(state), wallCache = getLabWallCache(state, context)) {\\n  if (labDisableDeadRoomDetection || modeMapOnly) return\\n  const routing = state?.routing\\n  if (!routing || !context) return\\n  for (const [frontierKey, stub] of routing.frontierStubs) {\\n    const parentRoom = state.rooms?.[stub.parentRoomKey]\\n    const frontierRoom = state.rooms?.[frontierKey]\\n    if (!parentRoom?.explored || !frontierRoom || frontierRoom.explored) continue\\n    const delta = labDirections[stub.finalDir]\\n    if (!delta) continue\\n    const targetX = parentRoom.x + delta.dx\\n    const targetY = parentRoom.y + delta.dy\\n    const sourceBounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(parentRoom.x, parentRoom.y, context)\\n    const targetInExitSubquadrant = isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(targetX, targetY, context)\\n    const isExitCell = state.layout\\n      && Number.isInteger(state.layout.finishX) && Number.isInteger(state.layout.finishY)\\n      && targetX === state.layout.finishX && targetY === state.layout.finishY\\n    const isDead = !isExitCell\\n      && sourceBounds\\n      && isLabCoordsInsideBounds(targetX, targetY, sourceBounds)\\n      && !targetInExitSubquadrant\\n      && !isLabPositiveExitGoalSubquadrantBranch(state, parentRoom.x, parentRoom.y, stub.finalDir, context, sourceBounds)\\n      && isLabBranchDeadByWallState(state, parentRoom.x, parentRoom.y, stub.finalDir, context, wallCache, {\\n        bounds: sourceBounds,\\n        targetX,\\n        targetY,\\n        forceRefresh: true\\n      })\\n    stub.deadBranch = !!isDead\\n    setLabFrontierDeadBranchStatus(state, frontierKey, stub.deadBranch)\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// Drop any frontier stubs whose underlying frontier room no longer exists\\n// and mirror the chosen stub's dead-branch flag onto the frontier room.\\n// Dead-branch detection is now refreshed incrementally when stubs are\\n// rebuilt instead of sweeping every stub on every recorded move.\\nfunction pruneLabRoutingFrontierStubs(state) {\\n  const routing = state?.routing\\n  if (!routing) return\\n  for (const key of Array.from(routing.frontierStubs.keys())) {\\n    const r = state.rooms?.[key]\\n    if (!r || r.explored) routing.frontierStubs.delete(key)\\n    else setLabFrontierDeadBranchStatus(state, key, routing.frontierStubs.get(key)?.deadBranch === true)\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// Top-level routing update: called from recordLabRoom AFTER the topology\\n// version has been bumped (so wall cache and quadrant bounds are fresh).\\nfunction updateLabRoutingForRoom(state, room, newBoundarySealed = false, addedWalls = true) {\\n  const diag = labDiagStart()\\n  const finish = (detail = \\\"\\\") => {\\n    const routing = state?.routing\\n    labDiagEnd(\\\"updateLabRoutingForRoom\\\", diag, `room=${room?.x},${room?.y} nodes=${routing?.nodes?.size ?? 0} edges=${routing?.edges?.size ?? 0} stubs=${routing?.frontierStubs?.size ?? 0}${detail ? ` ${detail}` : \\\"\\\"}`)\\n  }\\n  if (!room?.explored || !room.exits) { finish(\\\"skip\\\"); return }\\n  ensureLabRouting(state)\\n  const context = getLabInfluenceContext(state)\\n  if (!context) { finish(\\\"no-context\\\"); return }\\n  bumpLabRoutingRevision(state)\\n  const roomKey = getLabKey(room.x, room.y)\\n  const routing = state.routing\\n  const isNode = isLabRoutingNodeRoom(state, room, context)\\n  // If this room had previously been a corridor cell on some edge and is\\n  // now (re)classified as a node, that edge must be split.  Drop it; the\\n  // re-stitch below will re-create the two halves.\\n  const previousEdgeId = routing.roomToEdge.get(roomKey)\\n  if (previousEdgeId !== undefined) removeLabRoutingEdge(state, previousEdgeId)\\n  if (isNode) addLabRoutingNode(state, room, context)\\n  // Re-stitch corridors on every open exit from this room.\\n  rebuildLabRoutingEdgesAround(state, room, context)\\n  // The just-recorded room used to BE a frontier — the stub for it is now\\n  // stale.  Drop it.\\n  routing.frontierStubs.delete(roomKey)\\n  // Refresh frontier stubs for every open-exit neighbour of this room.  Any\\n  // unexplored neighbour now has at least one explored parent (this room),\\n  // so we can record an attach path for it.\\n  refreshLabRoutingFrontierStubsAround(state, room, context)\\n  const routingWallCache = getLabWallCache(state, context)\\n  refreshLabDeadBranchEdgesForRoom(state, room, context, routingWallCache)\\n  // Also refresh for each EXPLORED neighbour of `room` — moving from\\n  // corridor to node here may have changed THEIR attach paths.  (Only their\\n  // own open exits matter; we don't recurse further.)\\n  for (const [, delta] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    const nbX = room.x + delta.dx\\n    const nbY = room.y + delta.dy\\n    const nb = state.rooms?.[getLabKey(nbX, nbY)]\\n    if (!nb?.explored || !nb.exits) continue\\n    // The neighbour's frontier-parent status may have flipped now that this\\n    // room is explored.  If it WAS a node and is no longer, demote it (drop\\n    // the node + its incident edges) and re-stitch the corridor through it.\\n    // If it was NOT a node and now should be, promote it (split any edge it\\n    // sits on, add the node, re-stitch).\\n    const nbKey = getLabKey(nbX, nbY)\\n    const wasNode = routing.nodes.has(nbKey)\\n    const shouldBeNode = isLabRoutingNodeRoom(state, nb, context)\\n    if (wasNode && !shouldBeNode) {\\n      // Demote: remove node + its incident edges, then re-stitch.  The\\n      // adjacent corridor sweep will reform a single edge through it.\\n      const incident = Array.from(routing.nodeEdges.get(nbKey) ?? [])\\n      for (const eid of incident) removeLabRoutingEdge(state, eid)\\n      routing.nodes.delete(nbKey)\\n      routing.nodeEdges.delete(nbKey)\\n      rebuildLabRoutingEdgesAround(state, nb, context)\\n    } else if (!wasNode && shouldBeNode) {\\n      const prevEdgeId = routing.roomToEdge.get(nbKey)\\n      if (prevEdgeId !== undefined) removeLabRoutingEdge(state, prevEdgeId)\\n      addLabRoutingNode(state, nb, context)\\n      rebuildLabRoutingEdgesAround(state, nb, context)\\n    }\\n    refreshLabRoutingFrontierStubsAround(state, nb, context)\\n    refreshLabDeadBranchEdgesForRoom(state, nb, context, routingWallCache)\\n  }\\n  pruneLabRoutingFrontierStubs(state)\\n  if (newBoundarySealed) refreshLabDeadBranchStatusesForFrontierStubs(state, context, routingWallCache)\\n  // Wall-enclosure analysis against the routing tree's frontier set —\\n  // sealed-pocket detection feeds the routing picker.  THREE-LAYER\\n  // PER-STEP COST CONTROL:\\n  //   1. Skip entirely when the recorded room added no walls AND no\\n  //      boundary was sealed.  An all-exits-open junction adds nothing\\n  //      to the wall cache, so no enclosure result can have changed.\\n  //      This short-circuits most corridor-cell recordings during fast\\n  //      exploration runs.\\n  //   2. Otherwise, restrict the scan to quadrants whose walls actually\\n  //      changed: the just-recorded room's quadrant always, plus all\\n  //      four when a boundary was newly sealed (inferred walls cross\\n  //      both sides of the boundary).\\n  //   3. Within those local stubs, the per-edge flood-fill is memoised in\\n  //      labBranchEnclosureCache, so repeat queries are O(1).\\n  // Dead-branch state is updated incrementally above for the recorded room\\n  // and explored neighbours.  Avoid the old all-stub sweep here; on large\\n  // mazes it was the visible per-move stall.\\n  finish(`boundary=${newBoundarySealed} walls=${addedWalls}`)\\n  return\\n}\\n\\n// Find the routing-graph node nearest to (x, y) along the corridor through\\n// it.  If (x, y) itself is a node, returns (x, y).  If it's a corridor cell\\n// belonging to an installed edge, returns whichever endpoint is closer.\\n// Returns null if the room is unexplored or its corridor has no node yet.\\nfunction nearestLabRoutingNodeFromRoom(state, x, y) {\\n  ensureLabRouting(state)\\n  const routing = state.routing\\n  const key = getLabKey(x, y)\\n  if (routing.nodes.has(key)) return { nodeKey: key, dirs: [], rooms: [] }\\n  const edgeId = routing.roomToEdge.get(key)\\n  if (edgeId === undefined) return null\\n  const edge = routing.edges.get(edgeId)\\n  if (!edge) return null\\n  // Determine our index along the corridor and pick the closer endpoint.\\n  const idx = edge.rooms.indexOf(key)\\n  if (idx < 0) return null\\n  const distToA = idx + 1            // steps from `key` back to aKey\\n  const distToB = edge.rooms.length - idx  // steps from `key` to bKey\\n  if (distToA <= distToB) {\\n    // Walk back toward aKey.  Reverse direction sequence dirsFromA up to idx+1.\\n    const flippedDirs = edge.dirsFromA.slice(0, idx + 1).map((d) => labDirections[d]?.back ?? d).reverse()\\n    return { nodeKey: edge.aKey, dirs: flippedDirs, rooms: edge.rooms.slice(0, idx).reverse() }\\n  }\\n  // Walk forward toward bKey.\\n  const dirs = edge.dirsFromA.slice(idx + 1)\\n  return { nodeKey: edge.bKey, dirs, rooms: edge.rooms.slice(idx + 1) }\\n}\\n\\nfunction getLabRoutingEdgeFirstDir(edge, fromNodeKey, toNodeKey) {\\n  if (!edge) return \\\"\\\"\\n  if (edge.aKey === fromNodeKey && edge.bKey === toNodeKey) return edge.dirsFromA[0] ?? \\\"\\\"\\n  if (edge.bKey === fromNodeKey && edge.aKey === toNodeKey) {\\n    const lastForward = edge.dirsFromA[edge.dirsFromA.length - 1]\\n    return labDirections[lastForward]?.back ?? \\\"\\\"\\n  }\\n  return \\\"\\\"\\n}\\n\\nfunction getLabRouteRoomNextFromDirs(fromX, fromY, dirs = []) {\\n  const dir = Array.isArray(dirs) ? dirs[0] : \\\"\\\"\\n  if (!dir || !labDirections[dir]) return {}\\n  const routeRoomNext = {}\\n  routeRoomNext[getLabKey(fromX, fromY)] = dir\\n  return routeRoomNext\\n}\\n\\n// Dijkstra over the routing graph. Uses a small local min-heap so each\\n// worker expands reachable routing nodes instead of sweeping every node.\\n// Returns Map<nodeKey, { distance, prevKey, viaEdgeId }>.\\n//\\n// `options.allowGoalSubnet` — when false, refuses to expand into any node\\n// living in the goal (bottom-right) subnet.  This is what makes modeMapOnly\\n// effective for routed paths: once the gate is active, even the routing\\n// tree won't propose a path that crosses into goal territory, exactly the\\n// same way the route builders restrict graph expansion.\\nfunction labRoutingDijkstra(state, fromNodeKey, options = {}) {\\n  const diag = labDiagStart()\\n  ensureLabRouting(state)\\n  const routing = state.routing\\n  const distances = new Map()\\n  const previous = new Map()\\n  const firstSteps = new Map()\\n  const minQuadrantRanks = new Map()\\n  const finish = (result, detail = \\\"\\\") => {\\n    labDiagEnd(\\\"labRoutingDijkstra\\\", diag, `from=${fromNodeKey} nodes=${routing.nodes.size} edges=${routing.edges.size} reached=${result.distances?.size ?? 0}${detail ? ` ${detail}` : \\\"\\\"}`)\\n    return result\\n  }\\n  if (!routing.nodes.has(fromNodeKey)) return finish({ distances, previous, firstSteps, minQuadrantRanks }, \\\"missing-start\\\")\\n  const allowGoalSubnet = options.allowGoalSubnet !== false\\n  const context = options.context ?? getLabInfluenceContext(state)\\n  const cacheKey = `${routing.revision ?? 0}|${fromNodeKey}|${allowGoalSubnet ? 1 : 0}|${getLabInfluenceSignature(context)}`\\n  const cached = routing.routeCache?.get(cacheKey)\\n  if (cached) return finish(cached, \\\"cached\\\")\\n  // Even with the gate active, allow expansion through the start node when\\n  // it itself sits in the exit subquadrant (rare but possible if the\\n  // worker has already been physically pushed into it).  Otherwise the\\n  // worker would be unable to leave.\\n  const fromNode = routing.nodes.get(fromNodeKey)\\n  const fromInExitSub = !allowGoalSubnet && context && fromNode\\n    && isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(fromNode.x, fromNode.y, context)\\n  distances.set(fromNodeKey, 0)\\n  const fromBounds = context && fromNode ? getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(fromNode.x, fromNode.y, context) : null\\n  minQuadrantRanks.set(fromNodeKey, fromBounds ? getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(fromBounds, context) : Infinity)\\n  const visited = new Set()\\n  const heap = [{ key: fromNodeKey, dist: 0 }]\\n  const pushHeap = (entry) => {\\n    heap.push(entry)\\n    let i = heap.length - 1\\n    while (i > 0) {\\n      const parent = (i - 1) >> 1\\n      if (heap[parent].dist <= entry.dist) break\\n      heap[i] = heap[parent]\\n      i = parent\\n    }\\n    heap[i] = entry\\n  }\\n  const popHeap = () => {\\n    if (heap.length === 0) return null\\n    const top = heap[0]\\n    const tail = heap.pop()\\n    if (heap.length > 0 && tail) {\\n      let i = 0\\n      while (true) {\\n        const left = i * 2 + 1\\n        const right = left + 1\\n        if (left >= heap.length) break\\n        let child = left\\n        if (right < heap.length && heap[right].dist < heap[left].dist) child = right\\n        if (heap[child].dist >= tail.dist) break\\n        heap[i] = heap[child]\\n        i = child\\n      }\\n      heap[i] = tail\\n    }\\n    return top\\n  }\\n  while (heap.length > 0) {\\n    const next = popHeap()\\n    if (!next || visited.has(next.key)) continue\\n    const bestKey = next.key\\n    const bestDist = distances.get(bestKey)\\n    if (bestDist === undefined || next.dist !== bestDist) continue\\n    visited.add(bestKey)\\n    const edges = routing.nodeEdges.get(bestKey) ?? new Set()\\n    for (const edgeId of edges) {\\n      const edge = routing.edges.get(edgeId)\\n      if (!edge) continue\\n      const otherKey = edge.aKey === bestKey ? edge.bKey : edge.aKey\\n      if (visited.has(otherKey)) continue\\n      // modeMapOnly gating: refuse to step into a node that lives inside\\n      // the exit subquadrant unless we are stepping out of one.  The rest\\n      // of the goal quadrant is freely traversable in mapping mode.\\n      if (!allowGoalSubnet && context) {\\n        const otherNode = routing.nodes.get(otherKey)\\n        if (otherNode && isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(otherNode.x, otherNode.y, context) && !fromInExitSub) continue\\n      }\\n      const candidate = bestDist + edge.cost\\n      const known = distances.get(otherKey)\\n      if (known === undefined || candidate < known) {\\n        distances.set(otherKey, candidate)\\n        previous.set(otherKey, { prevKey: bestKey, edgeId })\\n        const carriedFirstStep = firstSteps.get(bestKey)\\n        firstSteps.set(otherKey, carriedFirstStep || getLabRoutingEdgeFirstDir(edge, bestKey, otherKey))\\n        const otherNode = routing.nodes.get(otherKey)\\n        const otherBounds = context && otherNode ? getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(otherNode.x, otherNode.y, context) : null\\n        const otherRank = otherBounds ? getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(otherBounds, context) : Infinity\\n        minQuadrantRanks.set(otherKey, Math.min(minQuadrantRanks.get(bestKey) ?? Infinity, otherRank))\\n        pushHeap({ key: otherKey, dist: candidate })\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return finish(rememberLabRoutingRoute(routing, cacheKey, { distances, previous, firstSteps, minQuadrantRanks }))\\n}\\n\\n// Translate a worker's \\\"I want to reach (toX, toY)\\\" request into a flat\\n// dir sequence using the routing graph.  Falls back to null when:\\n//   * either endpoint isn't on the routing graph yet (caller should use the\\n//     greedy goalforward fallback or choose another target),\\n//   * or the two endpoints are in different connected components of the\\n//     routing graph (target is unreachable through known nodes — caller\\n//     should treat the target as unreachable).\\nfunction getLabRoutedDistance(state, fromX, fromY, toX, toY, options = {}) {\\n  if (fromX === toX && fromY === toY) return 0\\n  const routing = state?.routing\\n  if (!routing) return Infinity\\n  const fromAttach = nearestLabRoutingNodeFromRoom(state, fromX, fromY)\\n  const toAttach = nearestLabRoutingNodeFromRoom(state, toX, toY)\\n  if (!fromAttach || !toAttach) return Infinity\\n  if (fromAttach.nodeKey === toAttach.nodeKey) return fromAttach.dirs.length + toAttach.dirs.length\\n  const context = options.context ?? getLabInfluenceContext(state)\\n  // Honour modeMapOnly: if the gate is active and the target sits inside\\n  // the EXIT SUBQUADRANT, refuse the route — the worker should be doing\\n  // mapping work elsewhere first.  The rest of the goal quadrant is\\n  // allowed.\\n  const allowGoalSubnet = options.allowGoalSubnet !== false\\n  if (!allowGoalSubnet && context) {\\n    const toRoom = routing.nodes.get(toAttach.nodeKey)\\n    if (toRoom && isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(toRoom.x, toRoom.y, context)) return Infinity\\n  }\\n  const route = labRoutingDijkstra(state, fromAttach.nodeKey, { allowGoalSubnet, context })\\n  const nodeDist = route.distances.get(toAttach.nodeKey)\\n  if (nodeDist === undefined) return Infinity\\n  return fromAttach.dirs.length + nodeDist + toAttach.dirs.length\\n}\\n\\n// =============================================================================\\n// Routing-table frontier accessors\\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// These let the picker answer \\\"is this frontier reachable / how far / which\\n// way?\\\" using ONLY the routing tree — no full-room travel map walk needed when\\n// the routing tree has the answer (which is \\\"100% accurate\\\" per the\\n// frontierStubs invariant: a stub exists IFF we know an explored path that\\n// ends one step away from the frontier).\\n//\\n// Whenever a routing answer is unavailable (rare: target frontier whose\\n// parent-side corridor still has no node) the caller can choose again; the\\n// common path skips whole-map traversal entirely.\\n// =============================================================================\\n\\n// Distance (in labyrinth steps) from (fromX, fromY) to a frontier room at\\n// (frontierX, frontierY) using the routing tree.  Returns Infinity if the\\n// frontier has no stub yet OR if the frontier's stub-node and the worker's\\n// nearest node are in different connected components.\\nfunction getLabRoutedFrontierDistance(state, fromX, fromY, frontierX, frontierY, options = {}) {\\n  const routing = state?.routing\\n  if (!routing) return Infinity\\n  const stub = routing.frontierStubs.get(getLabKey(frontierX, frontierY))\\n  if (!stub) return Infinity\\n  const fromAttach = nearestLabRoutingNodeFromRoom(state, fromX, fromY)\\n  if (!fromAttach) return Infinity\\n  if (fromAttach.nodeKey === stub.parentNodeKey) return fromAttach.dirs.length + stub.cost\\n  const allowGoalSubnet = options.allowGoalSubnet !== false\\n  const context = options.context ?? getLabInfluenceContext(state)\\n  const { distances } = labRoutingDijkstra(state, fromAttach.nodeKey, { allowGoalSubnet, context })\\n  const nodeDist = distances.get(stub.parentNodeKey)\\n  if (nodeDist === undefined) return Infinity\\n  return fromAttach.dirs.length + nodeDist + stub.cost\\n}\\n\\n// =============================================================================\\n// claimNearestRoutedFrontier — robust, never-give-up frontier claimer.\\n//\\n// The full picker (claimAdjacentLabRoom / claimLabRoom / getLabAdvanceTarget)\\n// has a long list of \\\"skip this candidate\\\" rules — competing claims, branch\\n// penalties, dead-branch checks, modeMapOnly gates, etc.  When ALL frontier\\n// candidates trip at least one of those skip rules the picker returns null\\n// and the worker would otherwise just stop moving.  That was the bug behind\\n// \\\"secondary worker does nothing on entry\\\" and \\\"worker won't start on\\n// arrival\\\": the regular picker's filters made it possible to refuse every\\n// candidate even when there was an obvious legal next move.\\n//\\n// This fallback walks the routing-tree frontierStubs directly.  For every\\n// reachable frontier we compute (cost, goalwardness) and pick the cheapest\\n// goalward stub; if none are goalward we pick the cheapest stub overall.\\n// We only check the things that REALLY mean \\\"can't be entered\\\":\\n//   * the stub's path is honoured in full (so wall-cache + dead-branch\\n//     filtering already happened when the stub was registered),\\n//   * the stub's subnet passes the modeMapOnly gate.\\n// We deliberately ignore branch-enclosure penalties and competing claims:\\n// those are heuristics, and refusing to move based on a heuristic is what\\n// gets workers stuck.  Worst case the worker takes a slightly worse route;\\n// that beats not moving at all.\\n// =============================================================================\\n// claimLabExitSubquadrantTurnback — modeMapOnly safety net.\\n//\\n// If a worker has somehow ended up inside the exit subquadrant while the\\n// modeMapOnly gate is still active (typical cause: a corridor crosses the\\n// subquadrant boundary inside an edge whose endpoint nodes both sit\\n// outside, so the worker physically walks through subquadrant cells while\\n// traversing a single routing edge), we force it to turn around at once.\\n// The worker's recorded `room.path` is the shortest known path from start\\n// to its current cell — walking it backward (each dir flipped) is\\n// guaranteed to retrace explored rooms, so we just unwind the path until\\n// the cursor lands outside the subquadrant.\\n//\\n// We do NOT take a claim on the destination (it's an explored room, no\\n// claim semantics apply); we just return a synthetic claim object whose\\n// travelPath is the dir array the worker should walk.  The main loop's\\n// walker consumes that the same way it does any other claim.\\nfunction claimLabExitSubquadrantTurnback(server, workerId, fromX, fromY, options = {}) {\\n  if (!modeMapOnly) return null\\n  const state = inProgress.get(server)\\n  if (!state?.rooms) return null\\n  const context = getLabInfluenceContext(state)\\n  if (!context || !shouldDeferGoalQuadrant(state, context)) return null\\n  if (!isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(fromX, fromY, context)) return null\\n  const room = state.rooms[getLabKey(fromX, fromY)]\\n  if (!Array.isArray(room?.path) || room.path.length === 0) return null\\n  let x = fromX, y = fromY\\n  const travelPath = []\\n  for (let i = room.path.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\\n    const back = labDirections[room.path[i]]?.back\\n    const delta = labDirections[back]\\n    if (!delta) break\\n    travelPath.push(back)\\n    x += delta.dx\\n    y += delta.dy\\n    if (!isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(x, y, context)) break\\n  }\\n  if (travelPath.length === 0) return null\\n  const destKey = getLabKey(x, y)\\n  const skipKeys = options.skipKeys instanceof Set ? options.skipKeys : null\\n  if (skipKeys?.has(destKey)) return null\\n  const destRoom = state.rooms[destKey]\\n  return {\\n    key: destKey,\\n    x,\\n    y,\\n    path: Array.isArray(destRoom?.path) ? destRoom.path.slice() : [],\\n    travelPath,\\n    routeRoomNext: getLabRouteRoomNextFromDirs(fromX, fromY, travelPath),\\n    fromX,\\n    fromY,\\n    influenceSignature: \\\"\\\"\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction claimImmediateGoalwardCrossing(server, workerId, fromX, fromY, options = {}) {\\n  const diag = labDiagStart()\\n  const finish = (result, detail = \\\"\\\") => {\\n    labDiagDecision(`gateway ${detail || (result ? \\\"claim\\\" : \\\"none\\\")}`)\\n    labDiagEnd(\\\"claimImmediateGoalwardCrossing\\\", diag, `${server} ${workerId} from=${fromX},${fromY}${detail ? ` ${detail}` : \\\"\\\"}`)\\n    return result\\n  }\\n  const state = inProgress.get(server)\\n  if (!state?.rooms) return finish(null, \\\"no-state\\\")\\n  const room = state.rooms?.[getLabKey(fromX, fromY)]\\n  if (!room?.explored || !room.exits) return finish(null, \\\"no-room\\\")\\n  const context = getLabInfluenceContext(state)\\n  const fromBounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(fromX, fromY, context)\\n  if (!context || !fromBounds) return finish(null, \\\"no-context\\\")\\n  const fromRank = getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(fromBounds, context)\\n  if (!Number.isFinite(fromRank) || fromRank <= 0) return finish(null, \\\"already-goal-quadrant\\\")\\n  const skipKeys = options.skipKeys instanceof Set ? options.skipKeys : null\\n  let best = null\\n  for (const [dir, delta] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    if (room.exits?.[dir] !== true) continue\\n    const nextX = fromX + delta.dx\\n    const nextY = fromY + delta.dy\\n    const nextKey = getLabKey(nextX, nextY)\\n    if (skipKeys?.has(nextKey)) continue\\n    if (!isLabCoordsInsideLayout(nextX, nextY, state.layout)) continue\\n    const nextBounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(nextX, nextY, context)\\n    if (!nextBounds || nextBounds.key === fromBounds.key) continue\\n    const nextRank = getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(nextBounds, context)\\n    if (nextRank >= fromRank) continue\\n    const nextPath = getLabPathAfterMove(room.path ?? [], dir)\\n    const nextRoom = ensureLabRoom(state, nextX, nextY, nextPath)\\n    if (nextRoom.explored) continue\\n    if (nextRoom.claimedBy && nextRoom.claimedBy !== workerId) {\\n      const claimant = state.workers?.[nextRoom.claimedBy]\\n      if (claimant) {\\n        const claimantDist = getLabWorkerDistanceToClaim(server, claimant, nextRoom)\\n        if (claimantDist < 1 || (claimantDist === 1 && !isLabWorkerAuthoritative(state, workerId, nextRoom.claimedBy))) continue\\n      } else {\\n        clearLabRoomClaim(state, nextRoom)\\n      }\\n    }\\n    const candidate = {\\n      dir,\\n      room: nextRoom,\\n      rank: nextRank,\\n      goalDist: getLabGoalDistance(state, nextX, nextY)\\n    }\\n    if (!best\\n      || candidate.rank < best.rank\\n      || (candidate.rank === best.rank && candidate.goalDist < best.goalDist)) {\\n      best = candidate\\n    }\\n  }\\n  if (!best) return finish(null, \\\"none\\\")\\n  assignLabRoomClaim(state, best.room, workerId)\\n  inProgress.set(server, state)\\n  return finish({\\n    key: getLabKey(best.room.x, best.room.y),\\n    x: best.room.x,\\n    y: best.room.y,\\n    path: Array.isArray(best.room.path) ? best.room.path.slice() : getLabPathAfterMove(room.path ?? [], best.dir),\\n    travelPath: [best.dir],\\n    routeRoomNext: getLabRouteRoomNextFromDirs(fromX, fromY, [best.dir]),\\n    fromX,\\n    fromY,\\n    influenceSignature: \\\"\\\",\\n    gateway: true\\n  }, `dir=${best.dir} target=${best.room.x},${best.room.y} rank=${fromRank}->${best.rank}`)\\n}\\n\\n// Per-worker modeMapOnly override.  When the picker can't find any\\n// goal-forward frontier for a worker we flip this on for that worker\\n// alone — it switches into mapping mode (dead-end-first, ignore the\\n// exit-subquadrant gate) until a normal forward-progress frontier\\n// becomes available again, at which point we clear the flag.  Stored\\n// on the worker record so it survives across iterations.\\nfunction isLabWorkerMapOnly(state, workerId) {\\n  return modeMapOnly || state?.workers?.[workerId]?.forceMapOnly === true\\n}\\n\\nfunction setLabWorkerMapOnly(state, workerId, value) {\\n  if (!state?.workers?.[workerId]) return\\n  state.workers[workerId].forceMapOnly = value === true\\n}\\n\\n// Compute the \\\"preferred quadrant\\\" for a worker's next move based on\\n// crew distribution and unexplored coverage.  Rules from user spec:\\n//   * Starting quadrant is excluded.\\n//   * If exactly one of the two adjacent (rank-1) quadrants is empty\\n//     of workers, prefer that one.\\n//   * If both adjacent quadrants have workers, prefer the quadrant\\n//     with the highest unexplored/worker ratio (across all non-start\\n//     quadrants).\\n//   * If no other workers exist, return null (no preference).\\n// Returns the bounds of the preferred quadrant or null.\\n// Helper: resolve a quadrant key (e.g. \\\"1,0\\\") back to its bounds object.\\nfunction getLabQuadrantBoundsByKey(boundsKey, context) {\\n  if (!boundsKey || !context) return null\\n  for (const sx of [0, 1]) for (const sy of [0, 1]) {\\n    const b = getLabQuadrantBounds(sx, sy, context)\\n    if (b?.key === boundsKey) return b\\n  }\\n  return null\\n}\\n\\n// Sticky per-worker quadrant assignment.  Once a worker commits to a\\n// quadrant (it picks a frontier living there), the assignment is set\\n// and held until that quadrant runs out of unexplored cells.  This is\\n// what stops the FIRST worker from backtracking when a SECOND worker\\n// joins: crew-redistribution rules only apply to UNASSIGNED workers,\\n// so an existing assignment is never overridden by changes to who\\n// else has joined the maze.\\nfunction getLabAssignedQuadrantBounds(state, workerId, context) {\\n  const ak = state?.workers?.[workerId]?.assignedQuadrant\\n  if (!ak) return null\\n  if (((state.quadrantUnexplored ?? {})[ak] ?? 0) <= 0) return null\\n  return getLabQuadrantBoundsByKey(ak, context)\\n}\\n\\nfunction setLabWorkerAssignedQuadrant(state, workerId, boundsKey) {\\n  if (!state?.workers?.[workerId] || !boundsKey) return\\n  state.workers[workerId].assignedQuadrant = boundsKey\\n}\\n\\nfunction clearLabWorkerAssignedQuadrantIfDone(state, workerId) {\\n  const w = state?.workers?.[workerId]\\n  if (!w?.assignedQuadrant) return\\n  if (((state.quadrantUnexplored ?? {})[w.assignedQuadrant] ?? 0) <= 0) {\\n    delete w.assignedQuadrant\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction getLabPreferredQuadrantForWorker(state, workerId, context) {\\n  if (!state?.workers || !context) return null\\n  // Drop any stale assignment (target quadrant fully mapped) before\\n  // computing.  Cheap O(1) check.\\n  clearLabWorkerAssignedQuadrantIfDone(state, workerId)\\n  // Sticky assignment wins, full stop.  As long as the worker's\\n  // assigned quadrant has any unexplored cells, that's where it\\n  // continues — no recomputation, no flip from new crew arriving.\\n  const sticky = getLabAssignedQuadrantBounds(state, workerId, context)\\n  if (sticky) return sticky\\n\\n  // Unassigned worker.  Distribute via crew + unexplored rules,\\n  // counting OTHER workers by ASSIGNMENT (not by position) so that an\\n  // assigned worker is treated as committed to its quadrant even if\\n  // it's mid-corridor crossing into it.  Workers without an assignment\\n  // are unaccounted — that's fine, they'll get one as soon as they\\n  // pick.\\n  const startBoundsKey = `${1 - context.goalSideX},${1 - context.goalSideY}`\\n  if (state.frontierIndexReady !== true) rebuildLabFrontierIndex(state)\\n  const unexploredByKey = state.quadrantUnexplored ?? {}\\n  const quads = []\\n  for (const sx of [0, 1]) for (const sy of [0, 1]) {\\n    const bounds = getLabQuadrantBounds(sx, sy, context)\\n    if (!bounds) continue\\n    if (bounds.key === startBoundsKey) continue\\n    quads.push({ bounds, workerCount: 0, unexploredCount: unexploredByKey[bounds.key] ?? 0 })\\n  }\\n  if (quads.length === 0) return null\\n  for (const [otherId, other] of Object.entries(state.workers)) {\\n    if (otherId === workerId || !other) continue\\n    const ak = other.assignedQuadrant\\n    if (!ak) continue\\n    const q = quads.find((qq) => qq.bounds.key === ak)\\n    if (q) q.workerCount++\\n  }\\n  let totalAssigned = 0\\n  for (const q of quads) totalAssigned += q.workerCount\\n  if (totalAssigned === 0) return null\\n  const rank1 = quads.filter((q) => q.bounds.key !== `${context.goalSideX},${context.goalSideY}`)\\n  const unstaffed = rank1.filter((q) => q.workerCount === 0)\\n  if (unstaffed.length === 1) return unstaffed[0].bounds\\n  if (unstaffed.length === 2) {\\n    const a = unstaffed[0], b = unstaffed[1]\\n    if (a.unexploredCount !== b.unexploredCount) return (a.unexploredCount > b.unexploredCount ? a : b).bounds\\n    return (getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(a.bounds, context) <= getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(b.bounds, context) ? a : b).bounds\\n  }\\n  // All rank-1 quadrants are staffed — third+ worker.  Highest\\n  // unexplored/assignment-count ratio wins across all non-start\\n  // quadrants, including the goal quadrant.\\n  let best = null\\n  let bestRatio = -1\\n  for (const q of quads) {\\n    const ratio = q.unexploredCount / Math.max(1, q.workerCount)\\n    if (ratio > bestRatio) { bestRatio = ratio; best = q }\\n  }\\n  return best?.bounds ?? null\\n}\\n\\nfunction claimNearestRoutedFrontier(server, workerId, fromX, fromY, options = {}) {\\n  const diag = labDiagStart()\\n  let candidateCount = 0\\n  const finish = (result, detail = \\\"\\\") => {\\n    const stubs = inProgress.get(server)?.routing?.frontierStubs?.size ?? 0\\n    labDiagDecision(`routed ${detail || (result ? \\\"claim\\\" : \\\"none\\\")} stubs=${stubs} candidates=${candidateCount}`)\\n    labDiagEnd(\\\"claimNearestRoutedFrontier\\\", diag, `${server} ${workerId} from=${fromX},${fromY} stubs=${stubs} candidates=${candidateCount}${detail ? ` ${detail}` : \\\"\\\"}`)\\n    return result\\n  }\\n  const state = inProgress.get(server)\\n  if (!state?.routing) return finish(null, \\\"no-routing\\\")\\n  const context = getLabInfluenceContext(state)\\n  const workerMapOnly = isLabWorkerMapOnly(state, workerId)\\n  const deferExitSub = workerMapOnly && shouldDeferGoalQuadrant(state, context)\\n  const fromAttach = nearestLabRoutingNodeFromRoom(state, fromX, fromY)\\n  if (!fromAttach) return finish(null, \\\"no-attach\\\")\\n  const dijkstra = labRoutingDijkstra(state, fromAttach.nodeKey, { allowGoalSubnet: !deferExitSub, context })\\n  const preferredBounds = getLabPreferredQuadrantForWorker(state, workerId, context)\\n  const preferredKey = preferredBounds?.key ?? \\\"\\\"\\n  const skipKeys = options.skipKeys instanceof Set ? options.skipKeys : null\\n\\n  // Determine the worker's \\\"back direction\\\" — the dir that would reverse\\n  // its last physical step.  Any route whose first step matches this dir\\n  // is a backtrack; we only fall back to those if every forward route is\\n  // exhausted.  The current room's `path` records the dir sequence the\\n  // worker walked from start to here, so its last entry is the dir we\\n  // arrived FROM.  Reverse it to get \\\"go back.\\\"\\n  const currentRoom = state.rooms?.[getLabKey(fromX, fromY)]\\n  const arrivalPath = currentRoom?.path\\n  const lastDir = Array.isArray(arrivalPath) && arrivalPath.length > 0 ? arrivalPath[arrivalPath.length - 1] : \\\"\\\"\\n  const backDir = labDirections[lastDir]?.back ?? \\\"\\\"\\n\\n  // Compute the FIRST physical step of the route from (fromX, fromY) to\\n  // the explored parent of `stub`.  Three cases:\\n  //   1. Worker is mid-corridor (fromAttach.dirs non-empty): first step is\\n  //      always the first dir in fromAttach.dirs (toward the attach node),\\n  //      regardless of which frontier we're aiming for.\\n  //   2. Worker is AT a routing node and the stub attaches to that same\\n  //      node: first step is the first dir of stub.dirsFromNode (or the\\n  //      finalDir if dirsFromNode is empty — adjacent frontier).\\n  //   3. Worker is at a node and the stub attaches to a different node:\\n  //      walk Dijkstra `previous` back from stub.parentNodeKey to fromNode\\n  //      to find the first edge from fromNode; first step is that edge's\\n  //      first dir, oriented from fromNode toward its neighbour.\\n  const firstStepForStub = (stub) => {\\n    if (fromAttach.dirs.length > 0) return fromAttach.dirs[0]\\n    if (stub.parentNodeKey === fromAttach.nodeKey) {\\n      return stub.dirsFromNode[0] ?? stub.finalDir ?? \\\"\\\"\\n    }\\n    return dijkstra.firstSteps?.get(stub.parentNodeKey) ?? \\\"\\\"\\n  }\\n\\n  // Ranking depends on mode:\\n  //   * Normal (modeMapOnly off): prefer the frontier closest to the exit\\n  //     corner.  This drives the worker to take any known boundary passage\\n  //     into a more goalward area immediately and, once inside the goal\\n  //     quadrant, to descend toward the exit cell.  Total route cost is\\n  //     the tiebreaker.\\n  //   * modeMapOnly on: INVERT the preference.  Prefer the frontier whose\\n  //     room is FARTHEST from the exit (favouring dead-ends and away-from\\n  //     -goal exploration), so every nook gets visited before we ever\\n  //     consider entering the exit subquadrant.\\n  // Selection has two distinct policies:\\n  //   * NORMAL (modeMapOnly off): forward-first.  A forward route (first\\n  //     step != backDir) ALWAYS beats any backward route, so the worker\\n  //     never reverses direction while there is unexplored ground ahead.\\n  //     Within each bucket: closest to the exit corner, then cheapest.\\n  //   * modeMapOnly ON: dead-end-first.  A dead-end-shaped frontier (one\\n  //     known entrance) ALWAYS beats any non-dead-end frontier even when\\n  //     reaching it requires walking back through the corridor we just\\n  //     came down.  This is necessary because the user-visible contract\\n  //     of modeMapOnly is \\\"every nook gets mapped\\\" — and from a worker\\n  //     standing mid-corridor, every dead-end pocket on a sibling branch\\n  //     is structurally a backtrack first.  Letting forward-first win\\n  //     would skip them all.  Within each bucket we still prefer forward\\n  //     over backward, then farthest from the exit, then cheapest.\\n  // Collect viable frontiers with their rank keys, then keep only the best\\n  // live and best dead-branch candidates.  This avoids sorting every\\n  // frontier on each worker turn; frontier stubs are authoritative enough\\n  // that the best routed candidate should materialise directly.\\n  const candidates = []\\n  for (const [frontierKey, stub] of state.routing.frontierStubs) {\\n    if (skipKeys?.has(frontierKey)) continue\\n    const r = state.rooms?.[frontierKey]\\n    if (!r || r.explored) continue\\n    if (deferExitSub && context && isLabInExitGoalSubquadrant(r.x, r.y, context)) continue\\n    let claimedByOther = \\\"\\\"\\n    if (r.claimedBy && r.claimedBy !== workerId) {\\n      if (state.workers?.[r.claimedBy]) claimedByOther = r.claimedBy\\n      else clearLabRoomClaim(state, r)\\n    }\\n    const nodeDist = stub.parentNodeKey === fromAttach.nodeKey ? 0 : dijkstra.distances.get(stub.parentNodeKey)\\n    if (nodeDist === undefined) continue\\n    const total = fromAttach.dirs.length + nodeDist + stub.cost\\n    if (claimedByOther) {\\n      const claimantDist = getLabWorkerDistanceToClaim(server, state.workers?.[claimedByOther], r)\\n      if (Number.isFinite(claimantDist)\\n        && (claimantDist < total || (claimantDist === total && !isLabWorkerAuthoritative(state, workerId, claimedByOther)))) continue\\n    }\\n    const goalDist = getLabGoalQuadrantBoundaryDistance(state, r.x, r.y)\\n    const frontierBounds = context ? getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(r.x, r.y, context) : null\\n    const quadrantRank = context && frontierBounds ? getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(frontierBounds, context) : 0\\n    // Preferred-quadrant match: 0 (best) when this frontier sits inside\\n    // the worker's preferred quadrant (computed from crew distribution\\n    // and unexplored ratios), 1 otherwise.  When no preference exists\\n    // (solo run, or both adjacent quadrants equal) every frontier is 0.\\n    const preferredMatch = !preferredKey || (frontierBounds && frontierBounds.key === preferredKey) ? 0 : 1\\n    // Lowest quadrant rank touched anywhere on the routed path from worker\\n    // to this frontier (including all routing-tree nodes traversed and the\\n    // frontier room itself).  When a candidate's path passes through a\\n    // more-goalward quadrant — including the case where the destination\\n    // is back in the current quadrant but the route goes via an\\n    // intermediate goalward subnet — we want it to outrank candidates\\n    // whose path stays entirely in less-goalward territory.  This is the\\n    // fix for \\\"secondary worker walks past the boundary corridor\\\": even\\n    // when the far-side frontier is claimed by the primary, frontiers\\n    // whose route TRAVERSES the boundary now win over in-quadrant\\n    // exploration that would step away from it.\\n    const routeMinQuadrantRank = Math.min(quadrantRank, dijkstra.minQuadrantRanks?.get(stub.parentNodeKey) ?? quadrantRank)\\n    const firstStep = firstStepForStub(stub)\\n    if (!firstStep) continue\\n    const isForward = !backDir || (firstStep && firstStep !== backDir)\\n    const isDeadEnd = labDisableDeadRoomDetection ? false : isLabFrontierDeadEndCandidate(state, r)\\n    // Explicit \\\"first physical step crosses out of the worker's current\\n    // quadrant into a more-goalward quadrant\\\" detector.  This is the\\n    // strongest possible signal that we should take this candidate\\n    // RIGHT NOW: the worker is one step from a known goalward crossing,\\n    // and any other candidate is at best routing through extra cells\\n    // before reaching a similar boundary.  Sorts ABOVE everything\\n    // except the dead-branch flag, so a discovered boundary-crossing\\n    // is taken on sight regardless of preferredMatch / tier / goalDist.\\n    let crossesGoalwardBoundary = false\\n    if (firstStep && context) {\\n      const fromBounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(fromX, fromY, context)\\n      const stepDelta = labDirections[firstStep]\\n      if (fromBounds && stepDelta) {\\n        const stepX = fromX + stepDelta.dx\\n        const stepY = fromY + stepDelta.dy\\n        const stepBounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(stepX, stepY, context)\\n        if (stepBounds && stepBounds.key !== fromBounds.key) {\\n          const stepRank = getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(stepBounds, context)\\n          const fromRank = getLabQuadrantGoalDistance(fromBounds, context)\\n          if (stepRank < fromRank) crossesGoalwardBoundary = true\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n    // Tier ordering (smaller = better):\\n    //   modeMapOnly: dead-end beats non-dead-end, forward beats backward\\n    //                inside each → 0=fwdDead, 1=bwdDead, 2=fwdOpen, 3=bwdOpen.\\n    //   normal:      forward beats backward → 0=fwd, 1=bwd; dead-end status\\n    //                is irrelevant inside each tier.\\n    let tier\\n    if (workerMapOnly) {\\n      tier = isDeadEnd ? (isForward ? 0 : 1) : (isForward ? 2 : 3)\\n    } else {\\n      tier = isForward ? 0 : 1\\n    }\\n    // Dead-branch stubs (entrance into a wall-proven enclosed pocket)\\n    // stay in the candidate list but rank LAST.  They're picked only\\n    // when nothing better is reachable — entering them adds at most\\n    // one room of map info and doesn't open new territory, but for a\\n    // stuck worker they're better than stalling.\\n    const deadBranch = labDisableDeadRoomDetection ? false : stub.deadBranch === true\\n    candidates.push({ frontierKey, stub, room: r, goalDist, total, quadrantRank, routeMinQuadrantRank, preferredMatch, tier, deadBranch, crossesGoalwardBoundary, claimedByOther })\\n  }\\n  candidateCount = candidates.length\\n  if (candidates.length === 0) {\\n    // No candidates at all in normal mode (e.g. every reachable\\n    // unexplored cell is claim-locked or behind the exit-subquadrant\\n    // gate).  Flip this worker into per-worker mapOnly and re-run; the\\n    // mapOnly variant has no exit-subquadrant gate, ranks dead-end\\n    // pockets, and frequently picks something the normal picker\\n    // wouldn't have considered.\\n    if (!workerMapOnly && state.workers?.[workerId]) {\\n      setLabWorkerMapOnly(state, workerId, true)\\n      inProgress.set(server, state)\\n      return finish(claimNearestRoutedFrontier(server, workerId, fromX, fromY, options), \\\"empty-rerun-mapOnly\\\")\\n    }\\n    return finish(null, \\\"empty\\\")\\n  }\\n  // Sort: mode-specific priority order.\\n  //   modeMapOnly: tier first (dead-end + forward priority), then within\\n  //                tier rank by farthest-from-exit and cheapest.  Quadrant\\n  //                rank is intentionally NOT used in mapping mode — we want\\n  //                full coverage of every region before moving on.\\n  //   normal:      ROUTE'S LOWEST QUADRANT RANK FIRST.  This wins over\\n  //                tier (forward/backward) AND over spread-distance, so:\\n  //                  - any frontier whose path passes through a more\\n  //                    goalward quadrant beats any frontier whose path\\n  //                    stays in less goalward territory,\\n  //                  - a backward step toward a known boundary corridor\\n  //                    beats forward in-quadrant exploration that would\\n  //                    walk past the corridor entrance,\\n  //                  - the spread-distance preference (which would push\\n  //                    secondary workers away from the primary, and\\n  //                    therefore away from the boundary the primary just\\n  //                    crossed) is overridden whenever it conflicts with\\n  //                    actual goalward progress.\\n  //                Destination quadrantRank is the secondary key so that\\n  //                a frontier ENDING in the goal quadrant beats one\\n  //                merely passing through it.  Tier and spreadDist still\\n  //                differentiate within the same quadrant tier.\\n  const compareRoutedCandidate = (a, b) => {\\n    // Dead-branch stubs always rank last, regardless of mode.  They're\\n    // last-resort options for workers with no other choice.\\n    if (a.deadBranch !== b.deadBranch) return a.deadBranch ? 1 : -1\\n    // STRONGEST SIGNAL: a candidate whose first physical step crosses\\n    // out of the worker's current quadrant into a more-goalward\\n    // quadrant is taken on sight.  This sits above EVERY other normal-\\n    // mode key (and modeMapOnly's tier ordering too — dead-end-first\\n    // is for full coverage of the current area, but a known goalward\\n    // crossing is more valuable still).  Without this an in-quadrant\\n    // candidate could occasionally edge out a one-step boundary cross\\n    // via cumulative tie-breakers (preferredMatch / goalDist / total)\\n    // even though routeMinQuadrantRank tied them at the same min rank.\\n    if (a.crossesGoalwardBoundary !== b.crossesGoalwardBoundary) return a.crossesGoalwardBoundary ? -1 : 1\\n    if (workerMapOnly) {\\n      if (a.tier !== b.tier) return a.tier - b.tier\\n      if (a.goalDist !== b.goalDist) return b.goalDist - a.goalDist\\n      return a.total - b.total\\n    }\\n    // INSIDE THE GOAL QUADRANT (both candidates have quadrantRank=0):\\n    // beeline to the exit corner.  goalDist beats tier here because\\n    // forward/backward heading is meaningless inside the small exit\\n    // subquadrant — the worker just needs to converge on (goalX, goalY).\\n    // Without this override the picker preferred a \\\"forward\\\" non-exit\\n    // cell over a \\\"backward\\\" step toward the exit, forcing the worker\\n    // to backtrack to find the exit cell at the end.\\n    if (a.quadrantRank === 0 && b.quadrantRank === 0) {\\n      if (a.goalDist !== b.goalDist) return a.goalDist - b.goalDist\\n      if (a.tier !== b.tier) return a.tier - b.tier\\n      return a.total - b.total\\n    }\\n    // Normal mode priority order outside the goal quadrant:\\n    //   1. routeMinQuadrantRank — paths through more goalward quadrants win.\\n    //   2. preferredMatch       — worker-assignment policy (un-staffed\\n    //                             rank-1 quadrant, or highest unexplored/\\n    //                             worker ratio) wins inside the same\\n    //                             route-rank tier.\\n    //   3. quadrantRank         — destination's quadrant for the final pick.\\n    //   4. tier                 — forward beats backward.\\n    //   5. goalDist             — closest to next-quadrant boundary.\\n    //   6. total                — cheapest route.\\n    if (a.routeMinQuadrantRank !== b.routeMinQuadrantRank) return a.routeMinQuadrantRank - b.routeMinQuadrantRank\\n    if (a.preferredMatch !== b.preferredMatch) return a.preferredMatch - b.preferredMatch\\n    if (a.quadrantRank !== b.quadrantRank) return a.quadrantRank - b.quadrantRank\\n    if (a.tier !== b.tier) return a.tier - b.tier\\n    if (a.goalDist !== b.goalDist) return a.goalDist - b.goalDist\\n    return a.total - b.total\\n  }\\n  let bestLiveCandidate = null\\n  let bestDeadCandidate = null\\n  for (const candidate of candidates) {\\n    if (candidate.deadBranch) {\\n      if (!bestDeadCandidate || compareRoutedCandidate(candidate, bestDeadCandidate) < 0) bestDeadCandidate = candidate\\n    } else if (!bestLiveCandidate || compareRoutedCandidate(candidate, bestLiveCandidate) < 0) {\\n      bestLiveCandidate = candidate\\n    }\\n  }\\n  // Helper: try to materialise a claim from a single candidate, returning\\n  // the claim object on success or null if path-planning failed.\\n  const tryClaim = (pick) => {\\n    assignLabRoomClaim(state, pick.room, workerId)\\n    if (!workerMapOnly) setLabWorkerMapOnly(state, workerId, false)\\n    if (!state.workers?.[workerId]?.assignedQuadrant && context) {\\n      const startKey = `${1 - context.goalSideX},${1 - context.goalSideY}`\\n      const pickBounds = getLabQuadrantBoundsForCoords(pick.room.x, pick.room.y, context)\\n      if (pickBounds && pickBounds.key !== startKey) setLabWorkerAssignedQuadrant(state, workerId, pickBounds.key)\\n    }\\n    inProgress.set(server, state)\\n    return {\\n      key: pick.frontierKey,\\n      x: pick.room.x,\\n      y: pick.room.y,\\n      path: Array.isArray(pick.room.path) ? pick.room.path.slice() : [],\\n      travelPath: [],\\n      routeRoomNext: getLabRouteRoomNextFromDirs(fromX, fromY, [firstStepForStub(pick.stub)]),\\n      allowGoalSubnet: !deferExitSub,\\n      deadBranch: pick.deadBranch === true,\\n      fromX,\\n      fromY,\\n      influenceSignature: \\\"\\\"\\n    }\\n  }\\n  // PASS 1: live (non-dead-branch) candidates only.  This is the\\n  // ordinary picker — pick the best live frontier whose path the\\n  // routing tree can build.\\n  if (bestLiveCandidate) {\\n    const claim = tryClaim(bestLiveCandidate)\\n    if (claim) return finish(claim, `${bestLiveCandidate.claimedByOther ? `takeover=${bestLiveCandidate.claimedByOther} ` : \\\"\\\"}live=${claim.x},${claim.y} total=${bestLiveCandidate.total}`)\\n  }\\n  // PASS 2: per-worker modeMapOnly fallback.  When no LIVE candidate\\n  // yielded a path AND we aren't already in mapOnly, flip in and recurse.\\n  // The worker's goal-forward progress is gated, so mapping mode (which\\n  // ignores the exit-subquadrant gate and ranks dead-end pockets first)\\n  // can find work the normal picker can't see.\\n  if (!workerMapOnly && state.workers?.[workerId]) {\\n    setLabWorkerMapOnly(state, workerId, true)\\n    inProgress.set(server, state)\\n    const mapOnlyResult = claimNearestRoutedFrontier(server, workerId, fromX, fromY, options)\\n    if (mapOnlyResult) return finish(mapOnlyResult, `mapOnly=${mapOnlyResult.x},${mapOnlyResult.y}`)\\n    // mapOnly turned up nothing — clear the override so we don't get\\n    // stuck in mapping mode after live frontiers reappear.\\n    setLabWorkerMapOnly(state, workerId, false)\\n    inProgress.set(server, state)\\n  }\\n  // PASS 3: dead-branch candidates — TRUE last resort.  Only entered\\n  // when both the live pass and the mapOnly fallback came up empty.\\n  // Walking into a dead-branch is wasteful (the pocket is wall-proven\\n  // enclosed and adds at most one room of map info) but it beats\\n  // stalling, and any progress somewhere else in the maze will\\n  // immediately surface a live frontier on the next pick.\\n  if (bestDeadCandidate) {\\n    const claim = tryClaim(bestDeadCandidate)\\n    if (claim) return finish(claim, `${bestDeadCandidate.claimedByOther ? `takeover=${bestDeadCandidate.claimedByOther} ` : \\\"\\\"}dead=${claim.x},${claim.y} total=${bestDeadCandidate.total}`)\\n  }\\n  return finish(null, \\\"none\\\")\\n}\\n\\n\\nfunction getLabKnownOrInferredPath(state, x, y) {\\n  const room = state?.rooms?.[getLabKey(x, y)]\\n  if (Array.isArray(room?.path)) return room.path.slice()\\n  const candidates = []\\n  for (const [dir, delta] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    const parentX = x - delta.dx\\n    const parentY = y - delta.dy\\n    const parent = state?.rooms?.[getLabKey(parentX, parentY)]\\n    if (!parent?.explored || parent.exits?.[dir] !== true || !Array.isArray(parent.path)) continue\\n    candidates.push(parent.path.concat(dir))\\n  }\\n  candidates.sort((a, b) => a.length - b.length)\\n  return candidates[0] ?? null\\n}\\n\\nfunction getLabCompletionDirFromState(state, x, y) {\\n  const room = state?.rooms?.[getLabKey(x, y)]\\n  const layout = state?.layout\\n  if (!room?.exits || !layout) return null\\n  for (const [dir, delta] of labDirectionEntries) {\\n    if (room.exits[dir] !== true) continue\\n    const nextX = x + delta.dx\\n    const nextY = y + delta.dy\\n    if (nextX < layout.minX || nextX > layout.maxX || nextY < layout.minY || nextY > layout.maxY) return dir\\n  }\\n  return null\\n}\\n\\nfunction getLabCompletionDir(server, x, y) {\\n  return getLabCompletionDirFromState(inProgress.get(server), x, y)\\n}\\n\\nfunction getLabFinishMarkerCoords(state) {\\n  const [finishRoomX, finishRoomY] = getLabAnchorCoords(state, \\\"finishKey\\\", state.layout?.finishX, state.layout?.finishY)\\n  if (!Number.isInteger(finishRoomX) || !Number.isInteger(finishRoomY)) return [null, null]\\n  const finishDir = getLabCompletionDirFromState(state, finishRoomX, finishRoomY)\\n  const finishDelta = finishDir ? labDirections[finishDir] : null\\n  if (!finishDelta) return [finishRoomX, finishRoomY]\\n  return [finishRoomX + finishDelta.dx / 2, finishRoomY + finishDelta.dy / 2]\\n}\\n\\n// Compute the maze-coordinate column / row of the four-way quadrant border\\n// from a layout.  The border falls on the WALL grid between the two pairs of\\n// quadrants — i.e. one step (in the appropriate parity sense) west of the\\n// right quadrant's first room column and one step north of the south\\n// quadrant's first room row.  Returns nulls when bounds aren't computable\\n// (e.g. estimated layout before the first room is recorded).\\nfunction getLabQuadrantBoundaryDisplayCoords(layout) {\\n  if (!layout) return [null, null]\\n  const minX = layout.minX, maxX = layout.maxX, minY = layout.minY, maxY = layout.maxY\\n  if (![minX, maxX, minY, maxY].every(Number.isFinite)) return [null, null]\\n  const midX = (minX + maxX) / 2\\n  const midY = (minY + maxY) / 2\\n  const xParity = ((layout.startX ?? minX) & 1)\\n  const yParity = ((layout.startY ?? minY) & 1)\\n  const rightMinX = midX + (((midX & 1) === xParity) ? 0 : 1)\\n  const southMinY = midY + (((midY & 1) === yParity) ? 0 : 1)\\n  // Wall column / row sit one step before the right/south quadrant's first\\n  // room column/row.  These are the cells we want darkened in the render.\\n  return [rightMinX - 1, southMinY - 1]\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildLabMapObject(state, forceRebuild = false) {\\n  if (!state?.layout) return []\\n  normalizeLabOrigin(state)\\n  syncLabAnchorLayout(state)\\n  const startCoords = getLabStartMarkerCoords(state)\\n  if (startCoords.length === 0) {\\n    const [startX, startY] = getLabAnchorCoords(state, \\\"rootKey\\\", state.layout?.startX, state.layout?.startY)\\n    if (Number.isInteger(startX) && Number.isInteger(startY)) startCoords.push([startX, startY])\\n  }\\n  const [finishX, finishY] = getLabFinishMarkerCoords(state)\\n  const [boundaryX, boundaryY] = getLabQuadrantBoundaryDisplayCoords(state.layout)\\n  const board = syncLabDisplayMap(state, forceRebuild)\\n  const anchorKey = `${startCoords.map((pair) => pair.join(\\\",\\\")).join(\\\";\\\")}|${finishX},${finishY}|${boundaryX},${boundaryY}`\\n  const outputKey = `${state.displayLayoutKey}|${state.displayVersion ?? 0}|${state.workersVersion ?? 0}|${anchorKey}`\\n  if (!forceRebuild && state.displayOutputKey === outputKey && Array.isArray(state.displayOutputRows)) return state.displayOutputRows\\n  const rows = buildLabMapOutput(board, state.layout, state.workers, { startCoords, finishX, finishY, boundaryX, boundaryY })\\n  state.displayOutputKey = outputKey\\n  state.displayOutputRows = rows\\n  return rows\\n}\\n\\n\\n\\nasync function getLabMapObject(ns) {\\n  const wrapRows = (rows, versionKey = \\\"\\\") => ({\\n    rows: Array.isArray(rows) ? rows : [],\\n    versionKey\\n  })\\n  if (labComplete !== false) {\\n    const completedMap = getLabCompleteMap()\\n    if (Array.isArray(completedMap) && completedMap.length > 0) return wrapRows(completedMap, `complete-map:${completedMap.length}:${completedMap[0]?.length ?? 0}`)\\n    const completedState = getLabCompleteState()\\n    if (completedState?.details?.modelId === \\\"(The Labyrinth)\\\" && completedState.rooms) {\\n      const rows = buildLabMapObject(completedState, true)\\n      return wrapRows(rows, `complete:${completedState.displayOutputKey ?? rows.length}`)\\n    }\\n  }\\n  if (labName) {\\n    const localState = inProgress.get(labName)\\n    if (localState?.details?.modelId === \\\"(The Labyrinth)\\\"\\n      && localState.layout\\n      && (localState.roomRecordCount ?? The original code\\n  // naively picked the closer endpoint and walked toward it — but that\\n  // ignores where the target actually is.  If the target lies past the\\n  // FARTHER endpoint, walking to the closer one sends the worker the wrong\\n  // way; once it arrives at the closer node, routing flips it back, and\\n  // it ping-pongs across the cell that closed the gap. proxy(ns, \\\"singularity.getCurrentWork\\\") : false\\n    if (SLEEVEACCESS && SLEEVES) await sleeveWork(ns)\\n\\n    if (me.city !== \\\"Sector-12\\\") {\\n      //Travel to our Gym\\n      clearLogs(ns)\\n      update(ns, \\\"Please go to Sector-12\\\")\\n      if (HASBN4) await travelCity(ns, \\\"Sector-12\\\")\\n      await ns.asleep(1000)\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    if (skls.strength < STATS) {\\n      clearLogs(ns)\\n      update(ns, \\\"Train Str to 30\\\")\\n      if (SLEEVEACCESS && SLEEVES) await displaySleeves(ns)\\n      if (wrk === null || (wrk && wrk.classType !== \\\"str\\\")) {\\n        await setGym(ns, \\\"Powerhouse Gym\\\", \\\"str\\\")\\n      }\\n      await ns.asleep(1000)\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    if (skls.defense < STATS) {\\n      clearLogs(ns)\\n      update(ns, \\\"Train Def to 30\\\")\\n      if (SLEEVEACCESS && SLEEVES) await displaySleeves(ns)\\n      if (wrk === null || (wrk && wrk.classType !== \\\"def\\\")) {\\n        await setGym(ns, \\\"Powerhouse Gym\\\", \\\"def\\\")\\n      }\\n      await ns.asleep(1000)\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    if (skls.dexterity < STATS) {\\n      clearLogs(ns)\\n      update(ns, \\\"Train Dex to 30\\\")\\n      if (SLEEVEACCESS && SLEEVES) await displaySleeves(ns)\\n      if (wrk === null || (wrk && wrk.classType !== \\\"dex\\\")) {\\n        await setGym(ns, \\\"Powerhouse Gym\\\", \\\"dex\\\")\\n      }\\n      await ns.asleep(1000)\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    if (skls.agility < STATS) {\\n      clearLogs(ns)\\n      update(ns, \\\"Train Agi to 30\\\")\\n      if   await ns.asleep(1000)\\n      continue\\n    }\\n  }\\n  if (HASBN4 && !NOTRAIN) await doCrime(ns, \\\"Homicide\\\") //Automatic switch to homicide\\n  //Do we have enough money, and are we bad   clearLogs(ns)\\n    update(ns, \\\"Do Homicide for Money and Karma\\\")\\n    update(ns, \\\"Join Slum Snakes when you can\\\")\\n    if (SLEEVEACCESS && SLEEVES) await displaySleeves(ns)\\n    if (HASBN4 && !NOTRAIN && wrk && wrk.crimeType !== \\\"Homicide\\\") {\\n      await doCrime(ns, \\\"Homicide\\\")\\n    }\\n    await ns.asleep(1000)\\n    continue\\n  }\\n  let count = 0\\n  let prev = 0\\n  let buf = \\\"\\\"\\n  //Are we in a gang yet?\\n  await gangCreate(ns, GANG_NAME)\\n  while (!haveGang) {\\n    if (HASBN4) await joinFac(ns, GANG_NAME)\\n    await gangCreate(ns, GANG_NAME)\\n    count--\\n    if (count < 0) {\\n      count = 30\\n      const karma = ns.heart.break()\\n      let result = 0\\n      if (prev === 0) {\\n        result = 0\\n      }\\n      else {\\n        result = ((-54000 - karma) / ((karma - prev) / 30)) * 1000\\n      }\\n      buf = ns.sprintf(\\\"Karma: %s / -54000  ETA: %s\\\", karma.toFixed(0), result === 0 ? \\\"n/a\\\" : fTime(ns, result))\\n      prev = karma\\n    }\\n    haveGang = await gangInGang(ns)\\n    clearLogs(ns)\\n    update(ns, buf)\\n    if (SLEEVEACCESS && SLEEVES) {\\n      await sleeveWork(ns)\\n      await \\\"Respect\\\"\\n  gangInfo = await gangGetGangInfo(ns)\\n  let oldPower = gangInfo.power\\n  let time = 0\\n  while (true) {\\n    memberNames = await gangGetMembers(ns)\\n    //if (MODE === \\\"Auto\\\" && memberNames.length === MAX_MEMBERS) MODE = \\\"Money\\\"\\n    fullMembers = await gangGetMembersFull(ns)\\n    gangInfo = await gangGetGangInfo(ns)\\n\\n\\n    otherGangInfo = await gangGetOtherGangInfo(ns)\\n    respectForNext = await gangRespectForNext(ns)\\n    if (SLEEVEACCESS && SLEEVES) await sleeveWork(ns)\\n    if (memberNames.length !== MAX_MEMBERS) await
